During the year ended December 31, 2025, the Company purchased and canceled an aggregate of 46,292 shares of Class A Stock for a total consideration of $3.0 million ($64.36 per share) under its share repurchase program.
On August 8, 2025, Oppenheimer filed a motion to dismiss the complaint on a number of grounds.
The Company reported a net loss of $20.6 million or $(1.93) per share (basic and diluted) for the first quarter of 2026, compared with net income of $30.7 million or $2.93 basic earnings per share for the first quarter of 2025."},{"url":"https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/OPY/10-k-oppenheimer-holdings-inc-files-annual-report-a2d780bb465a.html","title":"Oppenheimer outlines 2025 business and risks | OPY Annual Report (10-K)","content":"Inside Oppenheimer (NYSE: OPY) 2025 operations, assets and risks
Compensation represented 62.1% of 2025 revenue for the total firm, with 52.1% in Wealth Management and 60.9% in Capital Markets.
As of December 31, 2025, Oppenheimer reports client assets under administration of $143.3 billion.
We issued $14.2 million in forgivable notes (which are inherently illiquid) to employees for the year ended December 31, 2025 ($25.4 million for the year ended December 31, 2024) as upfront or backend inducements to commence or continue employment as the case may be.
